mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized broad-based demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or forward 60-day returns are negative in over 60% of synchronized expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, which reliably generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent alpha (interaction term p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all rolling windows, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 225, markets price in imminent monetary tightening and higher discount rates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 55% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates manufacturing strength offset by construction weakness, creating sector rotation that yields negative S&P 500 forward returns as growth concerns dominate.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) or forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, declining retail sales signal peak consumer spending and impending demand contraction, which triggers profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in over 55% of late-cycle retail sales declines, or the regime-conditional effect vanishes (interaction p > 0.05).
